rag_rules: Place input assumptions and output guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds or visible relations are available
Use only visible component type features in type-level contracts; avoid implementation-specific subcomponent paths
Write output dependency guarantees as relations between visible outputs and inputs in type annexes
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope; otherwise omit the clause
generic_rules: Use named constants or visible relations for concrete bounds in assumptions and guarantees
Avoid direct AADL property references in AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
Use proper type declarations for comparison expressions (bool for comparisons, int/real for arithmetic)
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Write range comparisons as `value_in >= lower_limit and value_in <= upper_limit`
Use `eq` for equation declarations in AGREE annexes
Place type-level contracts in component type annexes
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes
